plant_profile.history: Unique History: A highly sacred and valued herb of the Himalayas, traditionally included in the group 'Deepaniya' (appetizer) and 'Shulaprashamana' (pain-relieving). Mentioned in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita for its unmatched 'Jwarahara' (fever-relieving) and 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing) properties. It is spiritually associated with 'Sattva' (purity and clarity). It is an endangered species. Physical Characteristics: An annual herb growing in the temperate Himalayas (altitude 1500m to 3000m). It features broad, heart-shaped (cordate) lower leaves and narrow, amplexicaul upper leaves. The flowers are small, yellowish-green to bluish-purple, in terminal panicles. The whole plant is extremely bitter. It is an endangered species. Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Xanthones (Swerchirin, Mangiferin) and Secoiridoids (Swertiamarin). The presence of swerchirin and mangiferin is crucial for its anti-viral, anti-carcinogenic, and anti-hyperglycemic action on the immune system. Strict Standardizations: The whole plant is standardized in API based on its yellowish-green color and characteristic extremely bitter-biting taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<6%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>3%), and water-soluble extractive (>15%). HPLC is used to monitor 1% to 2% amarogentin content in commercial extracts.

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_modulation: Prominently pacifies Pitta and Kapha doshas. Due to its Atyanta-Tikta (extremely bitter) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is the most prestigious herb for 'Jwarahara' (antipyretic) and 'Raktaprasadana' (blood-soothing) therapies, clearing the fiery and agitated qualities of Pitta and the heavy qualities of Kapha through its systemic detoxifying property.

medicinal_uses.expert_antipyretic_specialist__jwarahara: Kiratatikta is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all types of chronic and intermittent fevers (Jwara). Its Atyanta-Tikta (extremely bitter) nature helps in instantaneously clearing 'Ama' (toxins) from the blood and providing systemic cooling. WHO-aligned research confirms its potent antimalarial and anti-viral properties.
medicinal_uses.blood_purification_and_skin_health: Its anti-inflammatory and blood-purifying properties help in treating chronic skin conditions like acne, psoriasis, and eczema. It is traditionally used to improve skin radiance and prevent age-related skin aging by providing systemic blood-purifying effects.
medicinal_uses.liver_and_spleen_stimulant: Acts as a powerful agent for managing liver health. It is traditionally used to treat jaundice and fatty liver by promoting healthy bile secretion and clearing congestion in the 'Yakṛt' (liver) system. It helps in reversing liver enzyme imbalances.
medicinal_uses.worm_and_internal_parasite_care: Its extremely bitter nature makes it incomparable for managing stubborn intestinal parasites and worms (Krimi) that thrive in a high-Kapha environment. It helps in cleared stagnant toxins from the gut.
medicinal_uses.digestive_and_metabolism_specialist: Its Tikta (bitter) and Laghu (light) nature helps in igniting the Jatharagni (digestive fire) and cleared stagnant food from the gut. It is traditionally used to manage 'Anaha' (bloating) and severe indigestion.
medicinal_uses.metabolic_support_and_diabetes: Its Tikta (bitter) and Katu (pungent) properties help in regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity through its systemic metabolic balancing property. Clinical studies confirm its hypoglycemic potential.
medicinal_uses.phytochemical_profile: Rich in Iridoid Glycosides (Swertiamarin, Gentiopicroside, Amarogentin), Xanthones (Swerchirin), and various Phenolic compounds. Amarogentin is considered the most bitter-tasting natural compound identified to date.

preparation_methods.whole_plant_powder__churna_: 1-3g of fine powder taken twice daily with honey or warm milk (as an Anupana) for managing fevers.
preparation_methods.whole_plant_decoction__kwatha_: 5g of dried plant boiled in 100ml water reduced to 25ml for chronic fever and liver congestion. Take twice daily before meals.
preparation_methods.cold_infusion__hima_: 10ml of infusion taken twice daily for managing blood pressure and cooling the body.
preparation_methods.sudarshan_churna: Combined with Ginger, Black pepper, Long pepper, and other herbs for systemic fever and immunity support.
preparation_methods.external_paste: Ground fresh plant or paste applied directly to localized inflammations (Mumps, boils, skin rashes) to reduce heat and swelling.

dosage.adults: Powder (Purified): 1-3 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Arishta: 15-30 ml; divided throughout the day before meals.
dosage.children: Generally safe (in very small doses). 25 mg to 125 mg of powder mixed with honey once or twice daily for respiratory and digestive protection.
dosage.elderly: Very low doses (500mg - 1g) are advisable initially, monitoring for any increase in dizziness or intestinal cramping.

contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with extreme exhaustion or low blood sugar (hypoglycemia) should use it in moderation as it has systemic blood-sugar-lowering effect. Avoid during active extreme diarrhea or dysentery due to its 'Sara' (flowing) nature.
side_effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness. Discontinue if extreme bitterness causes nausea.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diabetic or anti-hypertensive medications as it may potentiate their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.
safety.toxicity_notes: GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb with a history of use spanning millennia across various global traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and Himachali medicine) without documented toxicity.
